metadata.title: The AI power & grid bottleneck — transformers, turbines, interconnection, and the fuel behind it
metadata.status: facts from utility/industry/press; the 'power, not capital, is the binding constraint' framing is labeled interpretation (and aligns with power_adequacy P1)
metadata.frame: The AI build is power-constrained, not just capital-constrained. ~$650B+ of hyperscaler 2026 capex collides with multi-YEAR lead times for the physical grid: high-voltage transformers, gas turbines, interconnection, and the fuel (gas + Russian-gated HALEU). The binding constraints are physical and on a 3-5 year clock that does not match the 2026-2027 datacenter schedule. Overlay; excluded from the proofs but mirrors the machine-proven power_adequacy P1 (AI power demand > supply through 2028). Connects to energy_web, power_adequacy, fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap (the capex), geopolitics-russia-energy-arctic (HALEU), fin-coreweave-oracle.

the_collision: Alphabet/Amazon/Meta/Microsoft guided to >$650B of AI-infrastructure capex in 2026 alone - but nearly HALF of US data-center projects may be delayed or cancelled due to grid + component bottlenecks. The money is not the binding constraint; the physical grid is. status: fact (capex + delay warnings) + interpretation (the 'binding constraint' read).

the_bottlenecks.transformers: High-voltage transformer lead times went from ~50 weeks to ~127 weeks (some critical units 3-4 years); HV substations 3-5 years; the global HV-transformer shortage is expected to last to at least 2029. Domestic supply is insufficient, so developers IMPORT - including from China - adding a geopolitical-supply risk to the grid itself. status: fact.
the_bottlenecks.gas_turbines: Large gas turbines (the 'big three': GE Vernova, Siemens Energy, Mitsubishi) are effectively sold out for years - turbine backlogs push new firm gas capacity toward ~2028-2029, the same window as the datacenter demand. status: fact (directional; exact slots vary).
the_bottlenecks.interconnection: US interconnection queues delay projects for years; utilities warn of regional capacity shortages as early as 2026. status: fact.
the_bottlenecks.fuel: The 'clean firm power' answers both have chokepoints: nuclear/SMR fuel (HALEU) is ~Russia-gated (geopolitics-russia-energy-arctic; power_adequacy P2 UNSAT to ~2029), and gas needs turbines + pipelines that are themselves backlogged. status: fact.

the_workaround: Because the grid can't connect them in time, hyperscalers go 'BRING YOUR OWN POWER' (BYOP) - behind-the-meter on-site generation (gas turbines, fuel cells, nuclear restarts like Three Mile Island, SMRs). This bypasses the HV-transformer + interconnection bottleneck but (a) still needs turbines/fuel that are backlogged/gated, and (b) moves the AI build off the regulated grid into self-supplied power - privatizing the energy chokepoint. status: fact + interpretation.

most_stranded_ranking.summary: #50 - rank the hyperscalers/neoclouds by exposure to power-STRANDING: lots of announced compute vs little SECURED firm power, worst for those who LEASE and don't self-generate. 2026 capex + secured-power facts are fact; the ranking is a labeled interpretation.
most_stranded_ranking.the_tell: Nadella, on record: 'you may actually have a bunch of chips sitting in inventory that I can't plug in ... it's not a compute glut, but a power and warm-shell glut' - the binding constraint flipped from GPUs to POWER. status: fact.

ranking_most_to_least_exposed.secured_power: claims ~10 GW secured over 3yr, >90% PARTNER-funded; LEASES datacenters, does not self-generate; Abilene off-grid gas via Crusoe (29 GE Vernova turbines ~1 GW)
ranking_most_to_least_exposed.stranding_signal: scrapped a planned 600 MW Abilene expansion; thinnest balance-sheet cushion; single-customer (OpenAI) concentration

ranking_most_to_least_exposed.secured_power: only ~850 MW ACTIVE vs >3.1 GW CONTRACTED end-2025 (energization gap); pure lessee
ranking_most_to_least_exposed.stranding_signal: capex tied to contracts + circular Nvidia financing ($2B) = if power slips, contracted capex strands

ranking_most_to_least_exposed.secured_power: Constellation Three Mile Island / Crane 835 MW nuclear PPA (restart ~2028)
ranking_most_to_least_exposed.stranding_signal: STRONGEST direct evidence - Nadella 'chips I can't plug in'; TD Cowen ~2 GW lease walk-away (mostly non-binding LOIs per SemiAnalysis) + 1.5 GW self-build freeze - but strongest balance sheet

ranking_most_to_least_exposed.secured_power: MOST self-reliant near-term: Hyperion (Richland Parish LA) funds 10 Entergy gas plants >7 GW + 2.5 GW renewables; phase-1 ~1.5 GW late-2027, ~5 GW by 2030
ranking_most_to_least_exposed.stranding_signal: enormous CONCENTRATED single-site bet ($27B Blue Owl JV), but its risk is execution/permitting, not procurement

ranking_most_to_least_exposed.secured_power: best power-MATCHED: Talen/Susquehanna up to 1,920 MW nuclear through 2042, front-of-meter spring 2026; diversified sites; Trainium cuts capex
ranking_most_to_least_exposed.stranding_signal: customer-committed 'substantial portion' of 2026 capex - low near-term stranding

ranking_most_to_least_exposed.secured_power: most efficient TPU fleet + strong balance sheet; clean-power deals are LONG-dated/speculative (Kairos SMR first reactor ~2030, fleet 500 MW by 2035; Commonwealth fusion 200 MW early-2030s)
ranking_most_to_least_exposed.stranding_signal: lowest near-term stranding; relies on grid+PPAs until the SMR/fusion bets mature

most_stranded_ranking.read: The split is balance-sheet + self-generation. The LESSEES who don't generate (Oracle, CoreWeave) are most exposed - their announced capex strands if contracted power doesn't energize. The cash-rich self-generators (Meta gas, Amazon nuclear) carry execution risk, not procurement risk. Microsoft is the on-record proof that even the strongest balance sheet has GPUs it cannot power.

idle_gpu_cost_model.summary: #50 - quantify the cost of a GPU sitting idle (no power). The arithmetic is fact; the input ranges are sourced; the 'honest ~3yr economic life' is the contested assumption (cf. fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap).

inputs.gb200_nvl72_rack: ~$3.0M / rack, 72 Blackwell GPUs -> ~$41,700/GPU; rack power ~120-132 kW (NVIDIA nominal 120 kW)
inputs.useful_life: accounting 6yr (MSFT/AMZN/GOOG extended from 3-4) vs economic ~2-3yr (Burry; entropic half-life)
inputs.rental_opportunity_cost: neocloud on-demand H100 ~$1.5-2/GPU-hr; B200/GB200 ~$3/hr; hyperscaler on-demand H100 ~$7/hr (AWS ~$6.88, Azure ~$7-12)
inputs.cluster_power: a 100k-GB200 cluster needs ~150-180 MW for GPUs (~250-500 MW all-in; GPUs ~40% of datacenter power)

model.carrying_cost_per_gb200_gpu_per_day: $41,700 / 1,095 days (3yr) = ~$38.1 depreciation + 10% WACC ($41,700*0.10/365 = ~$11.4) = ~$49.5/GPU/day - ACCRUES whether the GPU runs or not
model.per_rack_per_day: ~$2,740 depreciation + ~$822 carry = ~$3,562/day per idle GB200 rack
model.forgone_revenue_per_gpu_per_day: at ~$3/GPU-hr neocloud = ~$72/GPU/day (the opportunity cost on top of carry)
model.accounting_understatement: at the 6yr accounting life carry drops to ~$30.4/GPU/day - i.e. the books show ~$30/day while the honest 3yr economic cost is ~$49.5/day; the ~$19/day gap IS the depreciation trap, made physical by idleness

stranded_100k_cluster.capital: 100k GPUs ~= 1,389 GB200 racks ~= $4.17B of fast-depreciating silicon
stranded_100k_cluster.carrying_burn_idle: ~$49.5/GPU/day * 100,000 = ~$4.95M/DAY (~$1.81B/yr) burned while the cluster sits unpowered
stranded_100k_cluster.incl_forgone_revenue: + ~$7.2M/day forgone rental = up to ~$12M/day total economic cost of a fully idle 100k-GB200 cluster

idle_gpu_cost_model.read: Power is on a 3-5yr delivery clock; GPUs depreciate on a ~2-3yr clock. A cluster that arrives before its power strands ~$5M/DAY in pure carry (up to ~$12M/day with forgone revenue) on a $4.2B asset that is aging out the entire time it waits. This is the depreciation trap (fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap) expressed physically: the asset decays whether or not it can be plugged in, so a power delay is a direct, compounding capital loss - and Nadella's 'chips I can't plug in' is that loss being taken in real time.
idle_gpu_cost_model.grade: arithmetic FACT; input ranges sourced; 3yr economic-life assumption CONTESTED

why_it_matters: (1) It is the physical mirror of the financial thesis: the $650B+ capex and the $1.4T of OpenAI compute commitments (fin-microsoft-openai) assume power that is on a 3-5 year delivery clock the 2026-2027 buildout cannot meet - so a large share of announced capacity is, like the marks, a promise ahead of deliverable substance. (2) It compounds the DEPRECIATION trap (fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap): GPUs that age in ~2-3 years sitting idle waiting for power they can't get is stranded, fast-depreciating capital. (3) It hands leverage to whoever controls turbines (a few firms), transformers (a strained global supply incl. China), and fuel (Russia/HALEU, gas) - the energy chokepoint behind the compute chokepoint.
